_meta.keyKnowledge: We explore the diversity of drama by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and consider culturally responsive approaches.

_source.w: We explore the diversity of drama by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples and consider culturally responsive approaches.
y: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander drama encompasses a rich diversity of storytelling traditions across hundreds of nations.
Culturally responsive engagement ensures we approach First Nations drama with the respect and understanding it deserves.
Understanding these traditions challenges us to think more deeply about the power of performance.
eg: We explore the diversity of dramatic and storytelling traditions across different First Nations communities.
We learn about cultural protocols for engaging with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander drama.
We investigate how contemporary First Nations playwrights and performers bring their stories to stage.
We discuss what culturally responsive approaches look like in practice.
We explore how drama carries cultural knowledge, law and responsibility.
We engage with resources created by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander theatre-makers.
